what_it_does: Renders a phase-gated review card for a surfaced build-intent item, emphasizing the working outcome and a live demonstration over the list of changed files.
observations: The card branches rendering by lifecycle phase class (bi-inbox, bi-running, bi-done, bi-back).
Completed builds headline a plain-language outcome and a '▷ show me' button that places a runnable node via onDemonstrate.
Changed files and the raw build summary are collapsed behind a 'for the record' disclosure and never headline the review.
Awaiting builds optionally render a BlastRadiusReach consent control and a ContextBundle, both wired to onNavigate.
Surfaced-back builds (scope-overrun / verify-fail / crash) lead with a plain-language why and offer no demo.
The card header calls onOpen(d.id) to compile the decision, mirroring inbox behavior.
Permission mode is surfaced only inside the collapsed technical record.
standouts: Demonstrate-first UX: the primary action for a done build is to see it run, not to inspect edits.
Consent-time subcomponents (blast radius + context bundle) appear only in the awaiting phase.
Surfaced-back failures are framed as actionable operator reasons rather than diffs or logs.
conventions: React functional component with destructured props and local useState for the disclosure.
CSS class names use a bi- prefix and a phase-derived class string.
data-ui-ref attribute marks the component for UI tracing.
Inline title attributes explain governance concepts (consequence class, scope, permission mode).
Conditional rendering is driven by phase.cls values.
concerns: The item prop d is typed as any, so payload shape is not checked at compile time.
onNavigate is optional but is passed directly to child components without a fallback, risking undefined calls.
phase.cls doubles as both a CSS class and a logic branch key, coupling styling to lifecycle semantics.
The changed-files list uses the file path string as a React key, which assumes paths are unique within a build.
There is no runtime validation of payload fields such as p.scope, p.context, or p.blast_radius.
notes: A phase-driven React card that makes the working outcome and a live demo the review headline, while keeping blast-radius approval, context bundle, and edit logs as consent-time or collapsed affordances.
questions: What is the exact runtime shape of d.payload, and which fields are guaranteed?
How does deriveOutcome map liveTypes to outcome.demoType?
What does onOpen do beyond 'compile this decision', and what identifier does it expect?
Is onNavigate required whenever blast_radius or context is present, and what happens if it is omitted?
What are all possible phase.cls values produced by buildPhase?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the code implements the demonstrate-first review pattern described in the header comment, with changed files kept secondary and consent-time context/blast-radius surfaced only for awaiting builds.
intention_signals: Make build review about verifying the working result, not reading diffs; give operators blast-radius and context visibility when approving; let completed builds prove themselves by placing a runnable node; keep technical audit trails available but out of the primary flow.
novelty: A build-intent card that deliberately suppresses the changed-files list from the primary review surface and instead offers a live 'show me' demonstration as the headline for completed builds.
contribution: A reusable, phase-aware presentation component that encodes the demonstrate-first review pattern: completed builds expose an outcome + demo affordance, surfaced-back builds expose a human-readable reason, and audit details remain accessible but secondary.
summary_for_embedding: React component BuildIntentCard renders a phase-gated build-intent review card emphasizing a demonstrate-first outcome, with collapsed changed-files record and consent-time blast-radius/context subcomponents.
intention_for_embedding: Provide an operator-facing review surface where the working result and a live demonstration matter more than the edit list, and where approval-time context and reach are visible.

symbols.does: Renders a phase-gated build-intent review card with an outcome-first, demonstrate-first UX.
symbols.description: BuildIntentCard is a React functional component that receives a surfaced build-intent item and callbacks, computes its lifecycle phase and outcome, and renders a card whose primary content depends on phase: awaiting/running builds show the spec and consent-time blast-radius/context; completed builds headline a plain-language outcome plus a 'show me' button that places a runnable node; surfaced-back builds show an actionable why; changed files and build log are always collapsed behind a secondary disclosure.
